title: Sarga 11
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: Themes: devotion, freedom from attachment, knowledge and discernment. The passage moves from “Sumali, having learned that these night-wanderers had obtained boons, rose up from Rasatala with his followers, abandoning all fear.” through “This city of Lanka, suitable for Rakshasas, was established by your intelligent brother, the lord of wealth (Kubera), and belongs to us.” through “Then, after some time, while Ravana was residing there, the night-wanderer (Prahasta) addressed Ravana, who had spoken such words.”

Themes: self-knowledge, devotion, mind and meditation. The passage moves from “Prahasta then spoke these humble and reasonable words.” through “You alone will not be able to reverse this established order, which has been practiced by both gods and asuras, so please heed my advice.” through “"O King, O gentle one, O sinless one, this city of Lanka, belonging to the great-souled Rakshasas, was established by you, but this is not…”

Themes: action and duty, devotion, freedom from attachment. The passage moves from “O unequalled in valor, if you would indeed give it to us today, my satisfaction would be complete, and my duty would also be fulfilled.” through “The god Vaishravana, who was skilled in speech, having heard Prahasta's words, replied to him.” through “Kubera further stated, "Let the city of Lanka, formerly inhabited by Rakshasas, be given to him; please tell me, O revered one, what I should do…”

Themes: action and duty, self-knowledge, devotion. The passage moves from “Thus addressed, the Brahmarshi Vishrava, the foremost among sages, spoke to Kubera, who stood with folded hands, saying, "Listen, O son, to my…” through “Its waters are also covered with white water lilies, blue lotuses, and other fragrant flowers.” through “That city of Lanka is now empty, as Kubera has abandoned it and departed; therefore, enter that city with us and govern according to your own…”

Themes: devotion. The passage moves from “Thus spoken to by Prahasta, the mighty Dashagriva entered the city of Lanka with his brothers, army, and followers.” through “After being crowned by the Rakshasas, Ravana then populated that city, and it became completely filled with night-wanderers resembling dark…” through “But then Kubera, out of respect for his father's words, established a city on the moon-bright mountain (Kailasa), adorned with well-decorated…”

verses.sanskrit: सुमाली वरलब्धांस्तु ज्ञात्वा चैतान्निशाचरान् । उदतिष्ठद्भयं त्यक्त्वा सानुगः स रसातलात् ।। 7.11.1 ।।
verses.translation: Sumali, having learned that these night-wanderers had obtained boons, rose up from Rasatala with his followers, abandoning all fear.

verses.sanskrit: मारीचश्च प्रहस्तश्च विरूपाक्षो महोदरः । उदतिष्ठन्सुसंरब्धाः सचिवास्तस्य रक्षसः ।। 7.11.2 ।।
verses.translation: Maricha, Prahasta, Virupaksha, and Mahodara, who were the greatly excited ministers of that Rakshasa (Sumali), also rose up.

verses.sanskrit: सुमाली सचिवैः सार्धं वृतो राक्षसपुङ्गवैः । अभिगम्य दशग्रीवं परिष्वज्येदमब्रवीत् ।। 7.11.3 ।।
verses.translation: Sumali, surrounded by his ministers and other chief Rakshasas, approached Dashagriva, embraced him, and spoke these words.

verses.sanskrit: दिष्ट्या ते वत्स सम्प्राप्तश्चिन्तितो ऽयं मनोरथः । यस्त्वं त्रिभुवनश्रेष्ठाल्लब्धवान्वरमुत्तमम् ।। 7.11.4 ।।
verses.translation: Fortunately, O son, this cherished desire of yours has been achieved, as you have obtained an excellent boon from the best of the three worlds (Brahma).

verses.sanskrit: यत्कृते च वयं लङ्कां त्यकत्वा याता रसातलम् । तद्गतं नो महाबाहो महद्विष्णुकृतं भयम् ।। 7.11.5 ।।
verses.translation: O mighty-armed one, the great fear caused by Vishnu, for which we abandoned Lanka and went to Rasatala, has now departed from us.

verses.sanskrit: असकृत्तद्भयाद्भीताः परित्यज्य स्वमालयम् । विद्रुताः सहिताः सर्वे प्रविष्टाः स्म रसातलम् ।। 7.11.6 ।।
verses.translation: Frightened repeatedly by that fear, all of us together abandoned our own abode and fled, entering Rasatala.

verses.sanskrit: अस्मदीया च लङ्केयं नगरी राक्षसोचिता । निवेशिता तव भ्रात्रा धनाध्यक्षेण धीमता ।। 7.11.7 ।।
verses.translation: This city of Lanka, suitable for Rakshasas, was established by your intelligent brother, the lord of wealth (Kubera), and belongs to us.

verses.sanskrit: यदि नामात्र शक्यं स्यात्साम्ना दानेन वानघ । तरसा वा महाबाहो प्रत्यानेतुं कृतं भवेत् ।। 7.11.8 ।।
verses.translation: O sinless and mighty-armed one, if it is not possible to bring it back by conciliation or by gifts, then it should be done by force.

verses.sanskrit: त्वं तु लङ्केश्वरस्तात भविष्यसि न संशयः । त्वया राक्षसवंशो ऽयं निमग्नो ऽपि समुद्धृतः । सर्वेषां नः प्रभुश्चैव भविष्यसि महाबल ।। 7.11.9 ।।
verses.translation: O dear and mighty one, you will undoubtedly be the lord of Lanka, for by you this Rakshasa race, even though submerged, has been uplifted, and you will indeed be the master of all of us.

verses.sanskrit: अथाब्रवीद्दशग्रीवो मातामहमुपस्थितम् । वित्तेशो गुरुरस्माकं नार्हसे वक्तुमीदृशम् ।। 7.11.10 ।।
verses.translation: Then Ravana spoke to his maternal grandfather, who was present, saying, "Kubera, the lord of wealth, is our preceptor; you ought not to speak such things."

verses.sanskrit: साम्नापि राक्षसेन्द्रेण प्रत्याख्यातो गरीयसा । किञ्चिन्नाह तदा रक्षो ज्ञात्वा तस्य चकीर्षितम् ।। 7.11.11 ।।
verses.translation: Even though the venerable maternal grandfather was rejected by the superior lord of Rakshasas (Ravana) with gentle words, the Rakshasa (grandfather) then said nothing, understanding Ravana's intention.

verses.sanskrit: कस्यचित्त्वथ कालस्य वसन्तं रावणं ततः । उक्तवन्तं तथा वाक्यं दशग्रीवं निशाचरः ।। 7.11.12 ।।
verses.translation: Then, after some time, while Ravana was residing there, the night-wanderer (Prahasta) addressed Ravana, who had spoken such words.

verses.sanskrit: प्रहस्तः प्रश्रितं वाक्यमिदमाह स कारणम् ।। 7.11.13 ।।
verses.translation: Prahasta then spoke these humble and reasonable words.

verses.sanskrit: दशग्रीव महाबाहो नार्हस्त्वं वक्तुमीदृशम् । सौभ्रात्रं नास्ति शूराणां शृणु चेदं वचो मम ।। 7.11.14 ।।
verses.translation: O mighty-armed Ravana, you ought not to speak such things, for brotherhood does not exist among heroes; therefore, listen to my words.

verses.sanskrit: अदितिश्च दितिश्चैव भगिन्यौ सहिते हि ते । भार्ये परमरूपिण्यौ कश्यपस्य प्रजापतेः ।। 7.11.15 ।।
verses.translation: Aditi and Diti, two sisters of supreme beauty, were indeed the wives of Prajapati Kashyapa.

verses.sanskrit: अदितिर्जनयामास देवांस्त्रिभुवनेश्वरान् । दितिस्त्वजनयत्पुत्रान्कश्यपस्यात्मसम्भवान् ।। 7.11.16 ।।
verses.translation: Aditi gave birth to the gods, who became the lords of the three worlds, while Diti bore sons who were also born from Kashyapa.

verses.sanskrit: दैत्यानां किल धर्मज्ञ पुरीयं सवनार्णवा । सपर्वता मही वीर ते ऽभवन्प्रभविष्णवः ।। 7.11.17 ।।
verses.translation: O knower of Dharma and O hero, this entire earth, with its forests, oceans, and mountains, was indeed the realm of the powerful Daityas.

verses.sanskrit: निहत्य तांस्तु समरे विष्णुना प्रभविष्णुना । देवानां वशमानीतं त्रैलोक्यमिदमव्ययम् ।। 7.11.18 ।।
verses.translation: However, the powerful Vishnu, having slain them in battle, brought this imperishable three worlds under the control of the gods.

verses.sanskrit: नैतदेको भवानेव करिष्यति विपर्ययम् । सुरासुरैराचरितं तत्कुरुष्व वचो मम ।। 7.11.19 ।।
verses.translation: You alone will not be able to reverse this established order, which has been practiced by both gods and asuras, so please heed my advice.

verses.sanskrit: एवमुक्तो दशग्रीवः प्रहृष्टेनान्तरात्मना । चिन्तयित्वा मुहूर्तं वै बाढमित्येव सो ऽब्रवीत् ।। 7.11.20 ।।
verses.translation: Thus addressed, Ravana, with his inner self delighted, thought for a moment and then said, "Very well."

verses.sanskrit: स तु तेनैव हर्षेण तस्मिन्नहनि वीर्यवान् । वनं गतो दशग्रीवः सह तैः क्षणदाचरैः ।। 7.11.21 ।।
verses.translation: Indeed, on that very day, the powerful Ravana, filled with that joy, went to the forest with those night-wanderers.

verses.sanskrit: त्रिकूटस्थः स तु तदा दशग्रीवो निशाचरः । प्रेषयामास दौत्येन प्रहस्तं वाक्यकोविदम् ।। 7.11.22 ।।
verses.translation: Then, Ravana, the night-wanderer, who was situated on Trikuta mountain, sent Prahasta, who was skilled in speech, as an envoy.

verses.sanskrit: प्रहस्त शीघ्रं गच्छ त्वं ब्रूहि नैर्ऋतपुङ्गवम् । वचसा मम वित्तेशं सामपूर्वमिदं वचः ।। 7.11.23 ।।
verses.translation: "O Prahasta, go quickly and tell the lord of wealth, the chief of the Rakshasas, these conciliatory words from me."

verses.sanskrit: इयं लङ्का पुरी राजन्राक्षसानां महात्मनाम् । त्वया निवेशिता सौम्य नैतद्युक्तं तवानघ ।। 7.11.24 ।।
verses.translation: "O King, O gentle one, O sinless one, this city of Lanka, belonging to the great-souled Rakshasas, was established by you, but this is not proper for you."

verses.sanskrit: तद्भवान्यदि नो ह्यद्य दद्यादतुलविक्रम । कृता भवेन्मम प्रीतिर्धुर्मश्चैवानुपालितः ।। 7.11.25 ।।
verses.translation: O unequalled in valor, if you would indeed give it to us today, my satisfaction would be complete, and my duty would also be fulfilled.

verses.sanskrit: स तु गत्वा पुरीं लङ्कां धनदेन सुरक्षिताम् । अब्रवीत्परमोदारं वित्तपालमिदं वचः ।। 7.11.26 ।।
verses.translation: Having arrived at the city of Lanka, which was well-protected by Kubera, the messenger then spoke these words to the most generous lord of wealth.

verses.sanskrit: प्रेषितो ऽहं तव भ्रात्रा दशग्रीवेण सुव्रत । त्वत्समीपं महाबाहो सर्वशस्त्रभृतां वर ।। 7.11.27 ।।
verses.translation: O one of good vows, O mighty-armed, O best among all weapon-bearers, I have been sent to your presence by your brother, Dashagriva.

verses.sanskrit: तच्छ्रूयतां महाप्राज्ञ सर्वशास्त्रविशारद । वचनं मम वित्तेश यद्ब्रवीति दशाननः ।। 7.11.28 ।।
verses.translation: O greatly wise one, O expert in all scriptures, O lord of wealth, please listen to my message, which Dashanana (Ravana) conveys.

verses.sanskrit: इयं किल पुरी रम्या सुमालिप्रमुखैः पुरा । भुक्तपूर्वा विशालाक्ष राक्षसैर्भीमविक्रमैः ।। 7.11.29 ।।
verses.translation: O wide-eyed one, this beautiful city was indeed formerly inhabited by Rakshasas of terrible valor, led by Sumali and others.

verses.sanskrit: तेन विज्ञाप्यते सो ऽयं साम्प्रतं विश्रवात्मज । तदेषा दीयतां तात याचतस्तस्य सामतः ।। 7.11.30 ।।
verses.translation: Prahasta requests Kubera, the son of Vishrava, to peacefully hand over Lanka to Ravana, who is now asking for it.

verses.sanskrit: प्रहस्तादभिसंश्रुत्य देवो वैश्रवणो वचः । प्रत्युवाच प्रहस्तं तं वाक्यं वाक्यविशारदः ।। 7.11.31 ।।
verses.translation: The god Vaishravana, who was skilled in speech, having heard Prahasta's words, replied to him.

verses.sanskrit: दत्ता ममेयं पित्रा तु लङ्का शून्या निशाचरैः । निवासिते च मे यक्षैर्दानमानादिभिर्गुणैः ।। 7.11.32 ।।
verses.translation: Kubera stated that his father had given him this Lanka, which was empty of Rakshasas, and he had settled it with Yakshas through various means like gifts and honors.

verses.sanskrit: ब्रूहि गच्छ दशग्रीवं पुरं राज्यं च यन्मम । तवाप्येतन्महाबाहो भुङ्क्ष्व राज्यमकण्टकम् ।। 7.11.33 ।।
verses.translation: Kubera instructed Prahasta to go and tell Ravana that the city and kingdom which were his, were now also Ravana's, and he should enjoy this unhindered kingdom, O mighty-armed one.

verses.sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा धनाध्यक्षो जगाम पितुरन्तिकम् ।। 7.11.34 ।।
verses.translation: Having spoken thus, Kubera, the lord of wealth, went to his father's presence.

verses.sanskrit: अभिवाद्य गुरुं प्राह रावणस्य यदीप्सितम् । एष तात दशग्रीवो दूतं प्रेषितवान्मम ।। 7.11.35 ।।
verses.translation: Having saluted his father, Kubera informed him of Ravana's demand, saying, "O father, Dashagriva has sent a messenger to me with his desire."

verses.sanskrit: दीयतां नगरी लङ्का पूर्वं रक्षोगणोषिता । मयात्र यदनुष्ठेयं तन्ममाचक्ष्व सुव्रत ।। 7.11.36 ।।
verses.translation: Kubera further stated, "Let the city of Lanka, formerly inhabited by Rakshasas, be given to him; please tell me, O revered one, what I should do in this situation."

verses.sanskrit: ब्रह्मर्षिस्त्वेवमुक्तो ऽसौ विश्रवा मुनिपुङ्गवः । प्राञ्जलिं धनदं प्राह शृणु पुत्र वचो मम ।। 7.11.37 ।।
verses.translation: Thus addressed, the Brahmarshi Vishrava, the foremost among sages, spoke to Kubera, who stood with folded hands, saying, "Listen, O son, to my words."

verses.sanskrit: दशग्रीवो महाबाहुरुक्तवान्मम सन्निधौ । मया निर्भर्त्सितश्चासीद्बहुशोक्तः सुदुर्मतिः ।। 7.11.38 ।।
verses.translation: Vishrava recounted that the mighty-armed, evil-minded Dashagriva had spoken to him in his presence, and he had repeatedly rebuked and admonished him.

verses.sanskrit: स क्रोधेन मया चोक्तो ध्वंसते च पुनः पुनः । श्रेयो ऽभियुक्तं धर्म्यं च शृणु पुत्र वचो मम ।। 7.11.39 ।।
verses.translation: Vishrava continued, "Though I spoke to him with anger, warning him that he would repeatedly perish, now listen, O son, to my words which are conducive to your welfare and righteousness."

verses.sanskrit: वरप्रदानसम्मूढो मान्या ऽमान्यान् स दुर्मतिः । न वेत्ति मम शापाच्च प्रकृतिं दारुणां गतः ।। 7.11.40 ।।
verses.translation: Bewildered by the boons, that evil-minded one does not distinguish between the respectable and the disrespectful, and furthermore, he has attained a terrible nature due to my curse.

verses.sanskrit: तस्माद्गच्छ महाबाहो कैलासं धरणीधरम् । निवेशय निवासार्थं त्यक्त्वा लङ्कां सहानुगः ।। 7.11.41 ।।
verses.translation: Therefore, O mighty-armed one, go to the mountain Kailasa, abandon Lanka, and establish your residence there with your followers.

verses.sanskrit: तत्र मन्दाकिनी रम्या नदीनामुत्तमा नदी । काञ्चनैः सूर्यसङ्काशैः पङ्कजैः संवृतोदका ।। 7.11.42 ।।
verses.translation: There flows the beautiful Mandakini river, the best among rivers, whose waters are covered with golden lotuses shining like the sun.

verses.sanskrit: कुमुदैरुत्पलैश्चैव तथा ऽन्यैश्च सुगन्धिभिः ।। 7.11.43 ।।
verses.translation: Its waters are also covered with white water lilies, blue lotuses, and other fragrant flowers.

verses.sanskrit: तत्र देवाः सगन्धर्वाः साप्सरोरगकिंनराः । विहारशीलाः सततं रमन्ते सर्वदा ऽ ऽश्रिताः ।। 7.11.44 ।।
verses.translation: Gods, along with Gandharvas, Apsaras, Uragas, and Kinnaras, constantly reside there, always delighting in recreation.

verses.sanskrit: न हि क्षमं तवानेन वैरं धनद रक्षसा । जानीषे हि यथा ऽनेन लब्धः परमको वरः ।। 7.11.45 ।।
verses.translation: O Kubera, it is indeed not proper for you to have enmity with this demon, for you know well how he has obtained a supreme boon.

verses.sanskrit: एवमुक्तो गृहीत्वा ऽ ऽशु तद्वचः पितृगौरवात् । सदारपुत्रः सामान्यः सवाहनधनो गतः ।। 7.11.46 ।।
verses.translation: Thus spoken to, Kubera, out of respect for his father, quickly accepted his words and departed with his wife, sons, retinue, vehicles, and wealth.

verses.sanskrit: प्रहस्तो ऽथ दशग्रीवं गत्वा वचनमब्रवीत् । प्रहृष्टात्मा महात्मानं सहामात्यं सहानुजम् ।। 7.11.47 ।।
verses.translation: Then Prahasta, with a joyful heart, went to the great-souled Dashagriva, who was with his ministers and younger brothers, and spoke these words.

verses.sanskrit: शून्या सा नगरी लङ्का त्यक्त्त्वैनां धनदो गतः । प्रविश्य तां सहास्माभिः स्वधर्मं प्रतिपालय ।। 7.11.48 ।।
verses.translation: That city of Lanka is now empty, as Kubera has abandoned it and departed; therefore, enter that city with us and govern according to your own duty.

verses.sanskrit: एवमुक्तो दशग्रीवः प्रहस्तेन महाबलः । विवेश नगरीं लङ्कां भ्रातृभिः सबलानुगैः ।। 7.11.49 ।।
verses.translation: Thus spoken to by Prahasta, the mighty Dashagriva entered the city of Lanka with his brothers, army, and followers.

verses.sanskrit: धनदेन परित्यक्तां सुविभक्तमहापथाम् । आरुरोह स देवारिः स्वर्गं देवाधिपो यथा ।। 7.11.50 ।।
verses.translation: Ravana, the enemy of the gods, ascended to that city (Lanka), which had well-divided great roads and had been abandoned by Kubera, just as Indra ascends to heaven.

verses.sanskrit: स चाभिषिक्तः क्षणदाचरैस्तदा निवेशयामास पुरीं दशाननः । निकामपूर्णा च बभूव सा पुरी निशाचरैर्नीलबलाहकोपमैः ।। 7.11.51 ।।
verses.translation: After being crowned by the Rakshasas, Ravana then populated that city, and it became completely filled with night-wanderers resembling dark clouds.

verses.sanskrit: धनेश्वरस्त्वथ पितृवाक्यगौरवान्न्यवेशयच्छशिविमले गिरौ पुरीम् । स्वलङ्कृतैर्भवनवरैर्विभूषितां पुरन्दरस्वेव तदा ऽमरावतीम् ।। 7.11.52 ।।
verses.translation: But then Kubera, out of respect for his father's words, established a city on the moon-bright mountain (Kailasa), adorned with well-decorated excellent mansions, just like Indra's Amaravati. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे एकादशः सर्गः ।। 11 ।। Thus ends the eleventh sarga of Uttara Kanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
